title: pedicularis gracilis subsp. brunoniana
intro: Pedicularis gracilis subsp. brunoniana, also known as Brunonian lousewort, is a plant species within the Pedicularis genus, which is a type of flowering plant in the bumblebee mint family, Orobanchaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. It is believed to have health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
